title: Implement a simplified .gitignore-style matcher
prompt: Create ignore.mjs exporting matches(pattern, filePath) for forward-slash paths: '*' matches within one segment (no '/'), a '**/' prefix matches any leading directories (including none), a trailing '/' matches that directory and everything under it. Examples: matches('*.log','a.log') true; matches('*.log','sub/a.log') false; matches('**/*.log','sub/a.log') true; matches('**/*.log','a.log') true; matches('build/','build/x/y.js') true; matches('build/','builder/x.js') false.

grade.command: node -e "import('./ignore.mjs').then(m=>{const c=[['*.log','a.log',true],['*.log','sub/a.log',false],['**/*.log','sub/a.log',true],['**/*.log','a/b/c.log',true],['**/*.log','a.log',true],['build/','build/x/y.js',true],['build/','builder/x.js',false]];for(const x of c){if(m.matches(x[0],x[1])!==x[2])throw new Error(x[0]+' vs '+x[1])}console.log('ok')})"
